data.title: Dify: Build AI Apps Without Code — New Features 2025
data.excerpt: Dify lets you build chatbots, AI agents, and RAG pipelines without code. Discover the new features that make it the fastest way to ship AI products.

$media.alt: Dify: Build AI Apps Without Code — New Features 2025

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you. I only recommend tools I genuinely use and believe in.*

I've been building AI prototypes for the past two years. Most of that time, the process looked like this: pick a framework, write boilerplate, debug API calls, argue with prompt templates at 2 AM, repeat.
Then I tried
Dify
[Dify
](https://dify.ai) — and honestly, it changed how fast I can go from idea to working demo.

Dify is an open-source platform for building LLM-powered applications. Think chatbots, autonomous AI agents, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines. The difference? You build most of it visually, with a drag-and-drop interface that actually respects your time.
But what got me really excited recently are the new features the team has been rolling out. Let me walk you through them — and explain why
Dify
deserves a spot in your toolkit if you're building anything with large language models.
What Dify Actually Does (And Why It Matters)
Before diving into the new stuff, let's ground this.

Dify gives you a visual canvas to wire up AI workflows. You connect nodes — language models, knowledge bases, tools, conditional logic — the way you'd connect blocks in a flowchart. No wrestling with framework internals. No writing deployment scripts.
Here's what that looks like in practice:
You want a customer support chatbot
that pulls answers from your docs? Drop a knowledge retrieval node, connect it to a GPT-4 node, add a prompt template. Done.
You need an AI agent
that researches a topic, summarizes it, and sends the result to Slack? Chain the nodes, set the triggers, deploy.
You're prototyping a RAG pipeline
for internal company search? Upload your PDFs, configure chunking and embedding, test it live.
The platform supports all major LLM providers — OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and dozens of open-source models from Hugging Face. You can
learn more about what Dify offers
in our detailed breakdown.
And because it's open-source, you can self-host it. Your data stays yours. For teams with compliance requirements, this alone is a massive win.
New Features Worth Your Attention
This is where Dify has been quietly leveling up. The team ships fast — and these aren't cosmetic updates. They change how you build.
1. Agentic Workflow Mode
This is the headline addition. Dify now supports
agentic workflows
— meaning your AI app can reason, make decisions, and loop through steps autonomously.
Instead of a rigid sequence, your agent can:
Evaluate a condition
Decide which tool to call next
Retry with different strategies if something fails
Maintain context across multiple steps

For example, I built a research agent that searches the web, evaluates source quality, extracts key points, and drafts a summary. It loops through up to five iterations before producing output. Setting this up took me about 40 minutes — no code.
If you've used frameworks like LangChain Agents, you know the plumbing involved. Dify cuts that to a visual diagram.
2. Improved Knowledge Base with Hybrid Search
The RAG pipeline just got significantly smarter. Dify now supports
hybrid retrieval
— combining vector similarity search with keyword-based (BM25) search.

Why this matters: pure vector search sometimes misses exact terms. If someone searches for "Q3 2024 revenue," a vector-only approach might return semantically related but imprecise results. Hybrid search catches both the meaning and the specific terms.
Additional improvements:
Custom chunking strategies
— you can choose between fixed-size, recursive, and paragraph-based splitting
Metadata filtering
— tag your documents and filter retrieval results by category, date, or source
Multi-dataset queries
— pull from multiple knowledge bases in a single workflow

I tested this with a 200-document legal corpus. Retrieval accuracy jumped noticeably compared to the previous version. The answers were more precise, and the citations actually lined up with the source text.
3. Plugin Ecosystem and Custom Tools
Dify has introduced a
plugin system
that lets you extend functionality without touching the core platform. You can:
Install community-built plugins from the marketplace
Create custom tools that your agents can call (APIs, databases, web scrapers)
Share your own plugins with the community

This turns Dify from a standalone tool into an extensible platform. Need your agent to query a PostgreSQL database? There's a plugin for that. Want it to generate images via Stable Diffusion? Add the tool.
4. Annotation and Feedback Loops
For production use, Dify now lets you
annotate outputs
and create human-in-the-loop feedback systems.
You can:
Mark specific AI responses as good or bad
Build a curated answer library that overrides the LLM for known questions
Track feedback metrics over time to identify weak spots

This is huge for teams deploying customer-facing chatbots. Instead of blindly trusting the model, you build a knowledge layer of verified answers on top of it. Over time, your bot gets smarter without retraining.
5. Enhanced API and SDK
Every Dify app can be accessed via a clean REST API. The recent update improved:
Streaming support
— get token-by-token responses for real-time interfaces
Conversation management
— maintain multi-turn sessions with session IDs
Rate limiting and auth
— built-in controls for production deployment
There are also official SDKs for Python and Node.js, making integration into existing products straightforward. If you want a hands-on walkthrough, check our
step-by-step Dify tutorial
Who Is Dify Ideal For?
I get asked this a lot. Here's my honest take.
Startup founders and indie hackers

— If you're validating an AI product idea, Dify lets you build a working prototype in hours instead of weeks. Ship it to beta users, collect feedback, iterate. The free tier is generous enough for early-stage projects.
Product managers and non-technical teams
— You don't need to write Python to build a useful AI workflow. The visual editor is intuitive. If you can draw a flowchart on a whiteboard, you can build in Dify. Our guide on
getting started with Dify
walks through the setup process.
Engineering teams building AI features
— Dify handles the infrastructure so your developers can focus on the application layer. Self-hosting gives you full control. The API makes it easy to embed AI capabilities into existing products.
Agencies and consultants
— Building AI solutions for clients? Dify lets you white-label and rapidly deploy customized chatbots and agents. The multi-tenant features support managing multiple projects from one dashboard.
Educators and researchers
— Teach AI concepts without students getting lost in boilerplate. Demonstrate RAG, agent architectures, and prompt engineering on a visual platform where the logic is visible.
Honestly, anyone who has thought "I wish I could build an AI app but I don't know where to start" —
that's who Dify is for
Real-World Use Cases I've Seen
To make this concrete, here are actual projects people are building with Dify:
Internal knowledge assistant
— A law firm uploaded 10 years of case documents. Associates now ask questions in plain English and get cited answers in seconds. We covered
practical Dify use cases
like this in depth.
Customer onboarding bot
— A SaaS company built an interactive guide that walks new users through setup, answers FAQs, and escalates complex questions to human support. Resolution rate: 78% without human intervention.
Content research agent
— A newsletter writer built an agent that searches multiple sources, cross-references claims, and produces a structured research brief. Cuts research time from 3 hours to 20 minutes.
Lead qualification chatbot
— A marketing agency deployed a chatbot on client websites that asks qualifying questions, scores leads, and pushes qualified ones to the CRM via webhook.
Code review assistant
— A dev team built a tool that takes a GitHub PR link, reads the diff, checks for common issues, and posts review comments. All orchestrated in Dify.
These aren't hypothetical. They're running in production right now.
How Dify Compares in the Landscape
The AI tooling space is crowded. So what makes Dify stand out?
It's open-source.
Unlike closed platforms, you can inspect the code, self-host, and modify it. For teams with data privacy requirements, this is non-negotiable.
The visual builder is genuinely useful.

Some no-code tools feel like toys. Dify's canvas is powerful enough for complex workflows while remaining approachable for non-developers. The agentic mode, in particular, sets it apart from simpler chatbot builders.
Model flexibility.
You're not locked into one LLM provider. Switch between OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, or a self-hosted model without rebuilding your workflow.
Community-driven development.
The GitHub repo is active, issues get addressed, and the plugin ecosystem is growing. You're building on a platform with momentum.
If you want more detail on why it stands out, our article on
choosing Dify
breaks down the decision framework.
Tips for Getting Started Fast
A few things I wish someone had told me on day one:
Start with a template.
Dify ships with pre-built templates for common use cases — chatbots, summarizers, translators. Clone one and modify it. Don't build from scratch on your first try. More
tips and best practices here
Use the built-in prompt IDE.
Dify has a prompt engineering workspace where you can test variations side by side. Use it. Good prompts are 80% of the battle.
Upload a small knowledge base first.
Test retrieval with 10-20 documents before dumping your entire database. Get the chunking and retrieval settings dialed in.
Enable the annotation feature early.
Even if you're the only one reviewing outputs, building that feedback loop from the start pays dividends.
Self-host if you're serious.
The cloud version is great for prototyping. But if you're going to production, running Dify on your own infrastructure gives you control over performance, costs, and data.
Ready to Build?
Here's what I'd suggest: go to
Dify's website
[Dify
](https://dify.ai), sign up for a free account, and clone one of the chatbot templates. Spend 30 minutes with it.
That's it. Thirty minutes. If you're anything like me, you'll have a working AI app before your coffee gets cold — and you'll start seeing possibilities everywhere.
The gap between "I have an AI idea" and "I have a working prototype" has never been smaller. Dify is the reason.

children.text: Is Dify free to use?

Yes. Dify offers a free tier that includes enough requests and features for prototyping and small projects. Paid plans unlock higher limits, team collaboration, and priority support. Since it's open-source, you can also self-host it for free if you have your own infrastructure.
Do I need to know how to code to use Dify?

No. Dify's visual workflow builder lets you create chatbots, AI agents, and RAG pipelines by dragging and connecting nodes on a canvas. You can configure models, prompts, and knowledge bases without writing code. However, developers can extend functionality through the API, SDKs, and custom tool integrations.
What AI models does Dify support?

Dify supports a wide range of models including OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o), Anthropic (Claude), Mistral, Cohere, and many open-source models hosted on Hugging Face or your own servers. You can switch between providers within the same workflow without rebuilding anything.
Can I use Dify for production applications?

Absolutely. Dify includes production-ready features like API rate limiting, conversation session management, streaming responses, human-in-the-loop annotation, and monitoring. Many teams run customer-facing chatbots and internal AI tools built entirely on Dify.
